mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in less than 50% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger negative risk sentiment and downward pressure on S&P 500 prices within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 65% of instances where UNRATE rises by over 0.2 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate earnings revisions upward and generating positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce a statistically significant positive mean return with p-value below 0.05 over subsequent 60-day windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices indicate expanding manufacturing capacity utilization, which translates to higher corporate profit margins and positive equity returns, but only during periods of stable inflation.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth yields negative or flat 20-day returns when CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.4 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth rates above 2.0 percent reflect robust macroeconomic expansion that supports equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns within one week of data release.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following GDP prints above 2.0 percent are negative in more than 55 percent of historical cases.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 190.0, markets price in aggressive monetary tightening that compresses equity multiples, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in more than 60 percent of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 190.0.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and declines in the unemployment rate confirm genuine labor market tightening, boosting consumer spending forecasts and driving positive equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE produces a negative mean 20-day return with statistical significance at p less than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production signals weakening supply-side momentum that reduces corporate revenue expectations, generating negative S&P 500 returns within one week, particularly during late-cycle phases.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO month-over-month changes are followed by positive 5-day returns in more than 70 percent of late-cycle observations.
